title: contract.v1 — the frontmatter of one decision record (eADR)
description: The per-instance state carried in a contract's YAML frontmatter: its eADR identifier, a short human title, where the decision sits in its lifecycle, the date it reached that state, and — only when it replaces an earlier decision — the eADR it supersedes. A contract is one architecturally significant decision, recorded once and kept forever; this schema governs only the frontmatter, while the prose body (Decision, Significance, Rationale, Anti-choice, Status, and optionally Supersedes) is the template's domain and is checked separately by the shape rule. The requirement that a superseding decision name what it supersedes, and the judgement of whether an anti-choice is genuine, are the contract surface's own concerns reviewed at the pull request, not encoded here. This file is authored reference data: in core the engine does not yet validate a contract's frontmatter against it (that needs a frontmatter reader that arrives with a later module); its own well-formedness, and a representative instance's conformance, are checked by the surface's unit tests.

id.description: The decision record's identifier: the letters 'eADR-' followed by a four-digit zero-padded number (eADR-0001, eADR-0002, …). The 'eADR-' prefix is the engine's, so an engine decision record never collides with a product's own ADR system; the four digits are the width the directory listing sorts on and set the one-project ceiling at eADR-9999, a deliberate bound matching the locked design's example. This is the bare identifier; the file is additionally slug-named (eADR-0001-a-short-slug.md), so the filename carries the slug while this id does not.

title.description: A short, plain-language label for the decision, written for a human scanning the index (for example, 'The validator is a thin core over a kind registry'). One line; no trailing punctuation needed.

status.description: Where this decision sits in the 'decision' lifecycle. proposed = drafted but not yet in force; accepted = in force and authoritative (the top authority tier); superseded = replaced by a later decision and kept for history, never deleted. There is deliberately no 'rejected' state — a rejected alternative is recorded as the Anti-choice inside the contract that prevailed.

date.description: The date this record reached its current status, written as a plain calendar date YYYY-MM-DD (for example 2026-06-02). The YYYY-MM-DD shape is what is actually enforced; the date format is only a hint.

supersedes.description: The eADR identifier this record replaces — present ONLY when this decision supersedes an earlier one. A first-of-its-kind decision supersedes nothing and omits this field entirely. When present, it pairs with a Supersedes section in the body; that pairing, and the rule that a superseding decision must name what it replaces, are reviewed at the pull request rather than enforced by this schema in core.
